title: New equidistribution estimates of Zhang type
partial_reason: 88 math envs in source; this DAG captures the spine (~25 nodes). Skipped enumeration of Section 4-5-6 individual remarks and definitions.

thm:1.1.summary: Bombieri-Vinogradov for densely-divisible smooth-modulus sums up to $x^\theta$ with $\theta = 1/2 + 7/300$. Polymath8a's headline equidistribution result, sharper than Zhang.

lem:1.3.summary: Crude divisor-function bounds: $\tau(d) \ll_\varepsilon x^\varepsilon$; controlled higher moments. Workhorse used pervasively.

lem:1.4.summary: GCD sum identities: $\sum_{k \le K} (k,q) \le K\,\tau(q)$, and dual versions. Combinatorial input for Type I/II sums.

claim:2.3.summary: Modified $\mathrm{MPZ}^{(i)}[\varpi,\delta]$ estimate restricted to the densely-divisible class $\mathcal{D}^{(i)}$. The structural target whose unconditional resolution yields thm:1.1.

thm:2.4.summary: $\mathrm{MPZ}^{(4)}[\varpi,\delta]$ holds when $600\varpi + 180\delta < 7$ (uses Deligne); $\mathrm{MPZ}^{(2)}[\varpi,\delta]$ holds when $168\varpi + 48\delta < 1$ (no Deligne). The key inequality that powers Polymath8b's unconditional bounded gaps.

thm:2.8.summary: New Type I, II, III estimates with explicit linear constraints on $(\varpi, \delta, \sigma)$: $54\varpi+15\delta+5\sigma<1$, $56\varpi+16\delta+4\sigma<1$, etc. The atomic equidistribution inputs that combine into thm:2.4.

thm:2.9.summary: Generalized Bombieri-Vinogradov for Dirichlet convolutions $\alpha \star \beta$ with Siegel-Walfisz $\beta$. Standard input.

thm:5.1.summary: Type I estimates: bounds on $\sum_{q,n} \alpha(n)\,\Delta(\cdot)$ under the Type I dispersion method. Reduced to incomplete Kloosterman / exponential sum bounds via thm:2.8 part 1.

thm:5.8.summary: Type II estimates: similar reduction via different combinatorial decomposition. Fed by thm:2.8 part 2.

prop:5.10.summary: $q$-van der Corput-style $A$-process estimate for incomplete Kloosterman sums on densely-divisible moduli. The numerical $56\varpi+16\delta+4\sigma<1$ line traces back to here.

prop:5.12.summary: $B$-process / Cauchy-Schwarz-completion combination on hyper-Kloosterman sums, giving the second Type I/II estimate.

prop:5.13.summary: Reduction of Type II sums to algebraic-geometry Kloosterman bounds — the place where Deligne's Weil II is invoked.

thm:6.8.summary: Bounds on hyper-Kloosterman sums over arithmetic progressions of moduli. Algebraic-geometric core; rests on Deligne's Riemann Hypothesis for Frobenius eigenvalues.

thm:6.15.summary: Sheaf-theoretic estimate for Birch sums. Refined Deligne input used to widen the Type III window.

thm:6.17.summary: Estimates for sums over hyper-Kloosterman sheaves twisted by Dirichlet characters.

thm:6.27.summary: Square-root cancellation for trace-function correlations on densely-divisible moduli. Polymath8a's deepest Deligne-dependent input.

thm:6.29.summary: Final Type III estimate combining 6.8/6.15/6.17/6.27 to close out the $600\varpi+180\delta<7$ inequality.

thm:7.1.summary: Dense divisibility inheritance lemma: dense divisibility of the moduli is preserved under the combinatorial reductions of Section 5.

prop:8.1.summary: Sieve corollary: assuming $\mathrm{MPZ}^{(4)}[\varpi,\delta]$ in a region, $\mathrm{DHL}[k,2]$ holds for explicit $k$. The sieve consumer of thm:2.4.

prop:8.4.summary: Combination of $\mathrm{MPZ}^{(4)}$ with Deligne-free $\mathrm{MPZ}^{(2)}$: gives the unconditional improvement that fed into Polymath8b.

edges.note: $\mathrm{MPZ}^{(4)}$ inequality is the level-of-distribution upgrade that gives $\theta = 1/2 + 7/300$.
MPZ inequality assembled from the three Type I/II/III estimates of thm:2.8.
Dense-divisibility inheritance ensures the Type estimates apply to the smooth moduli MPZ requires.
Generalized Bombieri-Vinogradov is the baseline being improved on.
Divisor moment bounds in error terms.
GCD sum identities in Type I rearrangements.

MPZ_target.description: claim:2.3 ($\mathrm{MPZ}^{(i)}[\varpi,\delta]$) plus the inequality theorems thm:2.4 and thm:2.9. The structural reductions.

type_I_II_III_decomposition.description: thm:2.8: package of three Type estimates with explicit linear-constraint regions in $(\varpi,\delta,\sigma)$. The combinatorial heart of the argument.

kloosterman_layer.description: Section 5 propositions: $q$-van der Corput, Cauchy-Schwarz, completion methods to reduce Type sums to incomplete Kloosterman / hyper-Kloosterman sums.

deligne_layer.description: Section 6: algebraic-geometric core. Hyper-Kloosterman bounds, sheaf-theoretic Birch sums, trace-function cancellation. All ride on Deligne's Weil II (Riemann Hypothesis over finite fields).

dense_divisibility_layer.description: thm:7.1: structural lemma showing the densely-divisible class is preserved through reductions.

sieve_consequences.description: Section 8: prop:8.1 / prop:8.4 turn the equidistribution upgrades into $\mathrm{DHL}[k,2]$ consequences. The sieve interface to Polymath8b.

technical_workhorses.description: lem:1.3, lem:1.4: divisor and GCD sum bounds used pervasively.

Zhang:2014.title: Bounded gaps between primes
Zhang:2014.role: predecessor — Polymath8a is the open re-derivation and optimization

Deligne:WeilII.title: La conjecture de Weil II
Deligne:WeilII.role: foundational — Riemann Hypothesis over finite fields, used in Section 6

FriedlanderIwaniec:1985.title: Incomplete Kloosterman sums and a divisor problem
